name: Raso and the Castro Celta Loop
name_original: Circular del Raso y el Castro Celta
description:
This circular route begins next to the El Cardenillo restaurant, which has a parking lot where you can leave your vehicle. It is from this same point that you cross the Roman bridge of Madrigal and begin an ascent toward the El Raso road.

After several intersections, you continue through pine forests toward the Las Planas area. Along the route, you alternate between sections of track and trail, passing through areas such as Los Reverteros, which follows the GR-180 for a short stretch. A detour along a path leads to the Castro Celta, which you can skirt and visit inside, stopping at its viewpoint. It's interesting to read about its history at this point while you hydrate and have a snack from your backpack.

From the fort, the route descends toward the village of El Raso, crossing the Vejiga stream. In the village, you can stop for a drink at the bar, if it's open.

The return journey follows the GR-180 and trails until it connects with the same outward route. Finally, a path leads you to the Alardos Gorge, which you cross by hopping over rocks before returning to the starting point. Don't forget to bring sunscreen if you decide to do the route in spring or summer, as well as a coat during the rest of the year.
region_intro: Hiking around Madrigal De La Vera offers access to diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos, featuring high mountain terrain, lush river valleys, and natural gorges. The region is characterized by granite peaks, clear natural pools, and varied vegetation including oak forests and poplar groves. Hikers can explore trails ranging from the challenging slopes of the Sierra de Gredos to the fertile plains of the Tiétar River valley. This area provides a range of physical features for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Madrigal De La Vera The most popular hiking route is Almanzor via the Tío Domingo path from the Hornillo Platform — round trip in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 8 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends towards the high mountain peaks of the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Raso and the Castro Celta Loop, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail explores the area around El Raso, including a Celtic fort, offering varied terrain and historical interest. Local hikers also love the Puente Pinillo - Hermanitos de Tejeda — Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, often completed in about 4 hours. This moderate route features prominent granite rock formations. Hiking around Madrigal De La Vera is defined by the granite peaks of the Sierra de Gredos, deep gorges with natural pools, and diverse forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging mountain ascents to more accessible valley walks. The routes in Madrigal De La Vera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Madrigal De La Vera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Madrigal de la Vera?
A: Madrigal de la Vera, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos, offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter abrupt, granite mountain peaks, lush river valleys with numerous gorges and natural pools, and varied vegetation including dense oak forests, poplar groves, and high mountain pastures. The region also provides panoramic views of the Tiétar Valley and the Rosarito Reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Madrigal de la Vera offers several accessible paths suitable for all skill levels. There are 12 easy routes available. Examples include the 'Ruta del Romartín' (approx. 10 km) and the 'Ruta del Helechoso' (approx. 7.5 km), which traverse pine and chestnut forests. A popular 11 km circular walk from Madrigal de la Vera also leads to the Roman Bridge and natural swimming pools, offering gentle terrain and scenic views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or natural swimming spots?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its 'gargantas' (gorges) like Garganta de Alardos and Garganta de Minchones, which feature crystal-clear natural pools ('charcos') and cascades. The spectacular Chorro de la Ventera, an 80-meter high waterfall in the Garganta de Minchones, is a rewarding destination for hikers. Many routes incorporate these natural swimming spots, perfect for a refreshing dip after a hike.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6316610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Madrigal de la Vera&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, often included in local walks. For those interested in history, the area around El Raso features a Celtic fort. In the higher mountains, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elola Refuge – Laguna Grande de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes and peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4095576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morezón Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/540292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Candeleda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Gredos offers demanding routes for experienced hikers. One such challenging trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380975672/almanzor-via-the-tio-domingo-path-from-the-hornillo-platform-round-trip-in-the-sierra-de-gredos-regional-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzor via the Tío Domingo path from the Hornillo Platform&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.8 km round trip with significant elevation gain towards the highest point in the Sierra de Gredos, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4618414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Almanzor&lt;/a&gt;. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380462844/pena-de-chilla-loop-from-el-raso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Chilla Loop from El Raso&lt;/a&gt;, covering 24.3 km with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Madrigal de la Vera?
A: The hiking routes in Madrigal de la Vera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to serene valley walks, and the abundance of natural features like gorges and natural pools.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359874886/raso-and-the-castro-celta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raso and the Castro Celta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.7 km path that explores the area around El Raso. There's also a popular 11 km circular walk from Madrigal de la Vera itself, leading to the Roman Bridge and natural swimming pools.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Madrigal de la Vera?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Madrigal de la Vera. Spring brings lush flowering vegetation, particularly along routes like the R11 from Puente Pinillo. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, but the natural pools provide opportunities for cooling off. Winter offers possibilities for snowshoeing in higher elevations of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Madrigal de la Vera's elevated position provides easy access to numerous viewpoints. Many trails offer spectacular panoramic vistas of the Tiétar Valley, the Sierra de Gredos, cultivated plains, and even the distant Montes de Toledo. Look for routes that ascend into the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos for the most expansive views.

Q: Are there any trails that feature unique rock formations?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Gredos is known for its impressive granite formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381031178/puente-pinillo-hermanitos-de-tejeda-parque-regional-de-la-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Pinillo - Hermanitos de Tejeda&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads through the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park and features the prominent granite rock formations known as the 'Hermanitos de Tejeda'.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in total around Madrigal de la Vera?
A: There are over 45 hiking routes available around Madrigal de la Vera, catering to various fitness levels. This includes 12 easy, 17 moderate, and 16 difficult trails, ensuring options for every type of hiker.
tips:
The Roman bridge of Madrigal, also known as the "Old Bridge," is a bridge of Roman origin that crosses the Alardos Gorge, a narrow valley carved by the Tiétar River. Currently, it consists of only one arch; its original form is unknown, as it underwent several reconstructions.
The bridge was built as part of the Roman road that linked Plasencia with Puerto del Pico and Ávila.
Almost touching the town of El Raso, close to the Mardos river valley. It represents an ancient population that had lived in this area of the peninsula called Vetons.

El Raso is a place steeped in ancient history, lush natural surroundings, and rural charm, ideal for those seeking a unique getaway. If you're passing through, take the opportunity to grab a drink in the village and take a stroll to see what it has to offer.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge of Madrigal de la Vera
